mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real retail and food services sales indicates expansion in consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives equity prices higher over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with month-over-month RSAFS changes (rolling 2-year rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls to or below 0.25%, the market has fully priced in aggressive accommodation, which historically signals an impending economic rebound and leads to positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Zero lower bound reached in December 2008
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than half of the triggered instances since the zero bound was reached.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous trough in housing starts and a peak in unemployment marks the turning point of the recession, as stabilization in housing and labor markets jointly indicate sustainable recovery, producing positive long-horizon equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST bottoms (two consecutive months of increase after a low) and UNRATE peaks (two consecutive months of decline after a high)
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of observations following the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (12-month CPI change below 1%), an increase in industrial production signals real economic growth without inflation pressure, which lifts equity valuations through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO changes on 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) during low inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production indicates supply-side expansion, which quickly bolsters investor confidence and leads to positive short-term equity returns within a week.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day correlation of INDPRO changes and forward 5-day returns is below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly GDP growth rate signals sustained economic expansion, which should lift corporate earnings and equity prices over the following quarter as the growth trajectory is confirmed.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not positively correlated with quarterly GDP growth rates (correlation < 0.05 over any 5-year window).

mechanism_narrative: When the central bank eases policy (low fed funds rate) and consumer spending simultaneously rises, the combined effect amplifies the positive outlook for equities above what either signal alone would suggest.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1% and RSAFS increasing for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 10% during a recession signals severe labor market distress that overwhelms any positive news, leading to negative equity returns as consumer spending and corporate earnings collapse.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 10%.
